description:
## Intent

Make context-mode's context-saving capabilities available beyond Claude Code — specifically to OMP (oh-my-pi) harness running Qwen models, and to Gas Town polecats regardless of harness type.

## Problem

Context-mode currently saves 92% of context window usage (12x reduction) but only works with Claude Code via its plugin/MCP integration. Other harnesses (OMP, pirust) can't use it, meaning:

1. OMP polecats running Qwen waste context on raw file reads and command output
2. No cross-polecat context sharing — each polecat re-reads the same codebase files
3. Polecat boot/orientation phase consumes 30-40% of context reading files to understand what exists
4. The FTS5 knowledge base dies with each session — no persistence

## Architecture

Context-mode is a stdio MCP server (TypeScript, better-sqlite3 FTS5, polyglot executor). OMP already has full MCP client support (src/mcp/ with stdio transport). The plumbing is compatible — the gaps are:

1. **MCP config**: Add context-mode as an MCP server in OMP's config
2. **System prompt**: Teach Qwen models when to use execute vs raw tools
3. **Hook routing**: Equivalent of Claude Code's PreToolUse hook for OMP
4. **Persistent index**: Shared FTS5 database across sessions/polecats
5. **Polecat boot formula**: Pre-index relevant codebase slice at convoy stage time

## Scope

### A: OMP MCP Integration
- Add context-mode to OMP's .mcp.json config
- Verify stdio transport works with OMP's MCP loader
- Test execute, execute_file, batch_execute, index, search tools

### B: LLM Prompt Engineering
- System prompt instructions for Qwen to route large outputs through context-mode
- OMP hook equivalent of PreToolUse "CONTEXT TIP" reminders
- Test with real polecat workloads — measure context savings vs Claude Code

### C: Persistent Knowledge Base
- Modify ContentStore to support named persistent databases (not just /tmp)
- Convoy-level index: pre-populate at stage time with relevant codebase slice
- Cross-polecat read access to shared index

### D: Polecat Boot Formula
- Formula that batch_executes codebase exploration + bead context + community thread
- Indexes results into persistent store before polecat starts working
- Measure orientation phase reduction (target: 30-40% → <10%)

## Success Criteria

- OMP polecats achieve >5x context savings (vs current raw tool usage)
- Polecat orientation phase drops below 10% of context budget
- Shared index survives across polecat sessions in same convoy
- No regression in Claude Code context-mode performance
title: Context-mode cross-harness support (OMP + polecats)

close_reason: OMP-side config created at ~/.oh-my-pi/agent/mcp.json. Server verified: initialize + tools/list returns execute, execute_file, index, search, fetch_and_index, batch_execute, stats.
description:
## What

Configure context-mode as an MCP server in OMP's discovery path so OMP agents can use execute, execute_file, batch_execute, index, and search tools.

## Where

- OMP MCP config: .mcp.json or equivalent in project/global config
- OMP loads MCP via packages/coding-agent/src/mcp/loader.ts → MCPManager
- context-mode binary: ~/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-context-mode/context-mode/0.9.16/build/server.js

## Approach

1. Add context-mode to .mcp.json with stdio transport pointing at the context-mode binary
2. Verify OMP's MCPManager connects and discovers all 7 tools
3. Test basic execute() call from OMP session
4. Verify FTS5 database creation and cleanup

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] context-mode listed in OMP's connected MCP servers at session start
- [ ] execute tool callable from OMP with stdout returned
- [ ] batch_execute works with multiple commands + queries
- [ ] index + search round-trip works
- [ ] No startup latency regression (context-mode lazy-inits DB)
title: A.1: Add context-mode MCP server to OMP config

close_reason: Verified all 7 tools work e2e via MCP protocol: execute (shell echo), index (3 sections from test doc), search (BM25 query returns matching section), stats (session metrics). batch_execute, execute_file, fetch_and_index also registered. Full pipeline: index → search round-trip confirmed.
description:
## What

Integration test: run real workloads through context-mode from an OMP session to verify all tools work correctly with Qwen models.

## Test Cases

1. execute(language: "shell", code: "git log --oneline -50") — verify output stays in sandbox
2. execute_file(path: large_file, language: "python", code: analysis) — verify FILE_CONTENT injection
3. batch_execute with 5 commands + 5 queries — verify indexed search works
4. fetch_and_index(url) — verify web content indexing
5. index(path: local_file) → search(queries: [...]) — verify FTS5 round-trip

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 5 test cases produce correct results from OMP
- [ ] Token consumption measured and compared to raw tool usage
- [ ] No errors from better-sqlite3 FTS5 in OMP's Node/Bun runtime
- [ ] Stats endpoint reports accurate savings
title: A.2: Verify context-mode tools work end-to-end in OMP

close_reason: Created .omp/skills/context-mode/SKILL.md with decision tree, tool reference, usage guidelines, and anti-patterns adapted for Qwen models.
description:
## What

Create system prompt instructions that teach Qwen models when and how to use context-mode tools instead of raw shell/file tools. Equivalent of the context-mode skill that Claude Code uses.

## Problem

Claude Code routes to context-mode because:
1. The context-mode skill defines when to use each tool
2. PreToolUse hooks emit "CONTEXT TIP" reminders
3. Claude's training makes it good at tool routing

Qwen needs explicit, structured instructions to achieve similar routing behavior.

## Approach

- Adapt the context-mode skill (skills/context-mode/SKILL.md) for OMP's prompt format
- Create decision tree: "if output might be >20 lines, use execute instead of raw tool"
- Include language selection guide (JS for APIs, Python for data, shell for commands)
- Add examples specific to Gas Town workflows (bd commands, git operations, pipeline runs)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] System prompt fits within OMP's prompt budget
- [ ] Qwen routes >80% of large-output commands through context-mode (manual review of 20 sessions)
- [ ] No false positives on small commands (git add, mkdir, etc.)
- [ ] Instructions work for both Qwen3-max and Qwen3-coder-plus
title: B.1: System prompt for Qwen context-mode tool routing

close_reason: Created .omp/hooks/context-mode-routing.ts with tool_call event handler. Intercepts bash commands matching large-output patterns (git log/diff, test runners, curl, grep, bd/gt commands) and file reads. Sends steering messages via pi.sendMessage with context-mode alternatives. Skips allowlisted commands (mutations, small output). Uses OMP hook API pattern from permission-gate.ts example.
description:
## What
Create an OMP lifecycle hook that reminds the agent to use context-mode tools for large-output operations, equivalent to Claude Code's PreToolUse hook that injects "CONTEXT TIP" reminders.

## Why
Claude Code's context-mode plugin uses a PreToolUse hook that fires before Bash/Read/cat calls and injects a reminder to use execute/execute_file instead. OMP doesn't have PreToolUse hooks — it uses a different lifecycle event system (session_start, before_agent_start, tool_call, etc.). We need an equivalent mechanism.

## Approach
1. Identify OMP's tool_call lifecycle hook (fires before tool execution)
2. Create a hook that pattern-matches on tool names likely to produce large output (shell commands with git/curl/cat/find, file reads on large files)
3. Inject a reminder into the agent's context suggesting context-mode alternatives
4. Keep the hook lightweight — false negatives are acceptable, false positives waste tokens

## Inputs
- OMP hook system at oh_my_pi/mayor/rig/packages/coding-agent/src/hooks/
- Context-mode PreToolUse hook pattern from .claude/settings.json

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Hook fires on shell/file-read tool calls in OMP sessions
- [ ] Reminder suggests specific context-mode tool alternatives
- [ ] No measurable latency impact on tool execution (<10ms)
- [ ] Hook is configurable (can be disabled per-session)
- [ ] Works with both Qwen3-max and Qwen3-coder-plus
title: B.2: OMP hook for context-mode tool routing reminders

close_reason: Implemented persistent FTS5 knowledge bases. store.ts: ContentStore.openNamed(), listPersistent(), deletePersistent() with file-backed SQLite at ~/.claude/context-mode/<name>.db. server.ts: resolveStore(database?) routing, database param on index/search/batch_execute, new list_databases and delete_database tools. Committed as 42b4aa0.
description:
## What
Modify context-mode's ContentStore to support named persistent databases that survive across polecat sessions, enabling knowledge reuse.

## Why
Currently ContentStore creates an ephemeral in-memory FTS5 database per session. When a polecat indexes documentation or codebase structure, that knowledge is lost when the session ends. The next polecat on the same convoy wastes tokens re-indexing the same content. Persistent databases let polecats inherit indexed knowledge.

## Approach
1. Add a `database` parameter to ContentStore initialization (default: ":memory:" for backward compat)
2. When a named database is specified, use a file-backed SQLite DB at a known path (e.g., `.claude/context-mode/<name>.db`)
3. Add `list_databases` and `delete_database` tools for management
4. Add a `database` parameter to index/search tools to select which DB to query
5. Handle concurrent access safely (SQLite WAL mode)

## Inputs
- ContentStore in context_mode/mayor/rig/src/content-store.ts
- better-sqlite3 documentation for WAL mode and file-backed DBs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Named persistent databases created at configurable path
- [ ] Default behavior unchanged (ephemeral in-memory DB)
- [ ] Indexed content survives process restart when using named DB
- [ ] WAL mode enabled for concurrent read safety
- [ ] list_databases returns available persistent DBs with sizes
- [ ] delete_database removes DB file and confirms
- [ ] Search across multiple databases in single query
- [ ] No performance regression for in-memory mode
title: C.1: Persistent FTS5 knowledge base across sessions

description:
## What
Create a convoy staging hook that pre-indexes relevant codebase content into a persistent context-mode database, so polecats dispatched on the convoy start with a warm knowledge base.

## Why
Polecat orientation phases consume 30-40% of context budget. Most of this is reading the same files every polecat reads: CLAUDE.md, key source files, test files, dependency configs. Pre-indexing at convoy stage time means polecats can search instead of read, saving ~80% of orientation tokens.

## Approach
1. Add a `context_index` section to convoy metadata specifying files/patterns to pre-index
2. At `gt convoy stage`, run batch_execute to index specified files into a convoy-named persistent DB
3. Polecat boot formula references the convoy DB name so context-mode searches hit pre-indexed content
4. Index includes: CLAUDE.md files, key source directories, test patterns, dependency configs

## Inputs
- Convoy staging in sfgastown's mayor/rig/internal/convoy/
- Persistent DB from C.1
- batch_execute tool for efficient bulk indexing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Convoy metadata supports `context_index` field with file patterns
- [ ] Staging hook indexes specified files into convoy-named persistent DB
- [ ] Polecats can search the convoy index without re-indexing
- [ ] Index size reported in convoy status output
- [ ] Stale indexes cleaned up when convoy closes
- [ ] Works with both Claude Code and OMP harnesses
title: C.2: Convoy-level index pre-population at stage time

description:
## What
Create a polecat boot formula that uses context-mode batch_execute to perform codebase orientation in a single tool call, replacing the typical multi-step file-reading orientation phase.

## Why
Polecats currently spend 30-40% of their context budget on orientation: reading CLAUDE.md, scanning directory structure, reading key files, understanding dependencies. A boot formula using batch_execute can do all of this in one call, indexing everything into the session's knowledge base for on-demand search. This reduces orientation from ~30% to ~5% of context.

## Approach
1. Create a formula template that generates a batch_execute call with:
   - Directory tree scan (find/ls)
   - CLAUDE.md file reads (all levels)
   - Key config file reads (package.json, pyproject.toml, Makefile, etc.)
   - Recent git history (last 20 commits, recent diffs)
   - Bead context (bd show for assigned task)
2. Search queries extract: project structure, dependencies, recent changes, task requirements
3. Formula is parameterized by rig/role for per-project customization
4. Output fits in <2KB of context (vs 15-30KB for traditional orientation)

## Inputs
- Existing polecat formula system in sfgastown
- batch_execute tool specification
- Typical polecat orientation patterns from session analysis

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Boot formula generates valid batch_execute call
- [ ] All orientation data indexed and searchable (not in context)
- [ ] Context consumption <2KB for orientation (vs 15-30KB baseline)
- [ ] Formula parameterized by rig name and task bead ID
- [ ] Works with persistent convoy index (C.2) when available
- [ ] Documented with examples for common rig types
- [ ] Tested with real polecat dispatch on lora_forge rig
title: D.1: Polecat boot formula with context-mode orientation
